subject: Mathematics
title: Zero: The Number That Changed Everything
description: A fast breakdown of Zero: The Number That Changed Everything for builders.

hook.text: For thousands of years, humans counted without zero. It simply didn't exist.

buildup.text: Ancient Babylonians used a placeholder, but Indian mathematicians made zero a true number.

discovery.text: Brahmagupta in 628 AD wrote rules for adding, subtracting, and multiplying with zero.

twist.text: Dividing by zero still breaks mathematics. It produces infinity or is simply undefined.

climax.text: Without zero, there's no binary code, no calculus, no modern math at all.

quiz.question: Who first treated zero as a true number with arithmetic rules?
